decisions.briefing: Markets slipped mildly today with both the S&P 500 and NASDAQ off -0.5%, a neutral-to-cautious session with no strong directional catalyst. Our cash sits at $477 — just below the $500 threshold required to open a new position — so we hold our six existing positions rather than force an undersized entry. The top momentum candidates (NAVN, USAR, LFTO, FPS) remain on close watch; any minor profit-taking or stop-loss exit from a current holding would immediately free up capital to deploy into them.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities edged lower on June 17 with the S&P 500 and NASDAQ each falling roughly 0.5%, reflecting a lack of fresh bullish catalysts and some routine profit-taking after recent gains. Markets broadly consolidated as investors digested mixed signals from the macroeconomic calendar and awaited further clarity on Federal Reserve policy direction. The mild pullback is consistent with a neutral tape — neither a sharp risk-off rotation nor a momentum-driven rally.
market_education.summary_zh: 美國股市於6月17日小幅收低，標普500指數與納斯達克指數各跌約0.5%，主因市場缺乏新的看多催化劑，加上近期累積漲幅引發部分獲利了結賣壓。投資人消化參差不齊的宏觀經濟數據，同時靜待聯儲局貨幣政策走向進一步明朗化。整體盤面偏向中性整固，既無明顯避險拋售，亦缺乏動能驅動的強勢上漲。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Dry powder refers to cash or liquid reserves held in a portfolio that are ready to be deployed into new investments at the right moment. Keeping some cash on hand means you can act quickly when strong opportunities appear — like our current momentum candidates — without being forced to sell other positions at an inopportune time. Today's session is a textbook example: our $477 cash reserve is just below the $500 floor we use, so we wait for a trigger (a sell or a slight cash top-up) rather than overextending.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「乾火藥」（Dry Powder）指投資組合中保留的現金或高流動性資產，隨時可在適當時機部署於新投資。持有一定現金緩衝的好處在於，當強勢機會出現時——如目前篩選出的高動能股票——能迅速出手，而無需在時機不佳時被迫賣出其他持倉。今日盤面正是典型示範：我們的477美元現金略低於500美元的入場門檻，因此選擇等待賣出信號或自然資金回補，而非勉強加倉。
